description: Fix binary data corruption in .docx ZIP extraction where DEFLATE-compressed entries are round-tripped through TextDecoder/TextEncoder, and fix Retry-After header parsing using wrong utility.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/microsoft-word && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix decompressDeflateRaw to return Uint8Array instead of string
userStories.description: The decompressDeflateRaw function converts decompressed bytes to a UTF-8 string via TextDecoder, which corrupts non-UTF-8 binary data (images, OLE objects). Change it to return raw Uint8Array bytes.
acceptanceCriteria: decompressDeflateRaw returns Uint8Array instead of string
extractAllZipEntries no longer round-trips through TextEncoder after decompression
extractTextFromDocumentXml still works correctly (it receives string content from the ZIP entry data decoded as needed)
Binary ZIP entries (images, etc.) are preserved byte-for-byte through decompression
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/microsoft-word/src/docx-utils.ts (fix decompressDeflateRaw and extractAllZipEntries)

Context:
The `decompressDeflateRaw` function (around line 60-86) decompresses data and returns a string:
```typescript
async function decompressDeflateRaw(compressed: Uint8Array): Promise<string> {
  const ds = new DecompressionStream('deflate-raw');
  // ... writes compressed data, reads chunks ...
  return new TextDecoder().decode(result);  // line 85 ŌĆö converts binary to UTF-8 string
}
```

In `extractAllZipEntries` (line 123+), this string is converted back to bytes:
```typescript
if (compressionMethod === 8) {
  const text = await decompressDeflateRaw(rawData);  // line 143
  data = new TextEncoder().encode(text);  // line 144 ŌĆö converts back to bytes
}
```

The TextDecoderŌåÆTextEncoder round-trip corrupts any non-UTF-8 bytes (images, binary OLE objects) because invalid byte sequences are replaced with U+FFFD.

Changes:
1. Change `decompressDeflateRaw` to return `Promise<Uint8Array>` ŌĆö remove the `TextDecoder().decode()` call and instead concatenate the chunks into a single Uint8Array directly
2. In `extractAllZipEntries`, remove the `TextEncoder().encode(text)` call ŌĆö just assign the Uint8Array directly: `data = await decompressDeflateRaw(rawData);`
3. Callers that need string content (like `extractTextFromDocumentXml`) should decode the Uint8Array to string at the call site using `new TextDecoder().decode(entry.data)`

Check all callers of decompressDeflateRaw to ensure they handle the Uint8Array return type. Also check all consumers of ZipEntry.data to ensure they decode to string where needed.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the ZipEntry interface ŌĆö `data: Uint8Array` is already the correct type
- The `extractTextFromDocumentXml` function takes a string argument ŌĆö callers must decode the entry data to string before passing it
- Do NOT change the DecompressionStream approach ŌĆö it's the correct API for browser-side DEFLATE decompression

userStories.title: Use SDK parseRetryAfterMs for Retry-After header parsing
userStories.description: Replace Number.parseInt for Retry-After header with the SDK's parseRetryAfterMs utility which handles both numeric seconds and HTTP-date formats.
acceptanceCriteria: The Retry-After header parsing uses parseRetryAfterMs from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk
parseRetryAfterMs is imported from the SDK
Number.parseInt is no longer used for Retry-After parsing
